response_parsed.topic: How to manage and dealing with fake content on social media.
response_parsed.opinion:
I've never actually dealt with this issue because I don't use social media at all. My anxiety keeps me away from it, and honestly, I'm not sure why anyone would want to be on there given how much fake stuff is everywhere. As a divorced Native American man living in Kansas City with five kids, I spend most of my time working part-time as a waiter or waitress and taking care of the family. We don't have formal education, but my daughter's mom has a master's degree so she can help us out there if needed. Social media just doesn't seem worth it to me, especially when you're dealing with all that fake content.

I guess I could try to understand what people who do use social media are going through. Fake news and misleading posts must be pretty frustrating for them. But since I don't have any exposure myself, I can only imagine the chaos. Maybe they need to be more aware of their own biases or double-check everything before believing it. Like when you're serving customers at a busy restaurant, sometimes you just need to ignore the noise and focus on your job.

But with my high neuroticism, I'd probably get pretty stressed if I had to deal with fake content constantly. It doesn't help that I'm low in agreeableness, so I wouldn't hesitate to call out someone spreading misinformation even if it means getting into arguments. Still, without any personal experience or using social media myself, my thoughts on this are probably not very helpful.
response_parsed.reasoning_summary: Based on the persona's traits and background (high neuroticism, anxiety leading to avoidance of social media), I derived that direct engagement with fake content is likely unappealing. The lack of formal education but reliance on family support suggests limited access to tools for combating misinformation. As a part-time waiter/waitress in Kansas City with five children, the persona focuses on practical tasks rather than theoretical discussions about social media management.
response_parsed.tone: Cautious and hesitant due to high neuroticism
